hadiths.chapter: 71. CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS FROM THE TIME OF ADAM
hadiths.ar:
5404  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْمُّعَّل َّى ن ْن ُّ مُّحَّم َّذٍ إَّلْن َّصْزِى  ُّ عَّن ْ ج َّعْق َّزِ ن ْن ِ سُّلَّي ْمَّان َّ عَّن ْ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ إَّلْحَّكَّمِ عَّن ْ إ ن ِي هِ عَّن ْ سَّعِي ذِ ن ْن ِ 
ج ُّن َّي ْزٍ عَّن ِ إِن ْن ِ عَّن  َّاسِ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ اً وَّصِي ِّى  وَّ ج َّلِي ق َّي ِى  وَّ ر َّوْج َّي َّهُّ ق َّاطِمَّه َّ سَّي ِّذَّة َّ 
ن ِسَّاءِ إَّلْعَّالَّمِي ن َّ إِن ْن َّي ِى  وَّ إَّلْحَّسَّن َّ وَّ إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن َّ سَّي ِّذَّإ س َّن َّاب ِ إ َّهْلِ إَّلْح َّن  َّه ِ وَّلَّذَّإى َّ مَّن ْ وَّإلأَّهُّمْ  ق َّق َّذْ وَّإلأَّن ِى  وَّ مَّن ْ عَّاذَّإهُّمْ 
ق َّق َّذْ عَّاذَّإن ِى  وَّ مَّن ْ ن َّاوَّإ َّهُّمْ  ق َّق َّذْ ن َّاوَّإ َّن ِى  وَّ مَّن ْ ج َّق َّاهُّمْ ق َّق َّذْ ج َّق َّان ِى  وَّ مَّن ْ ن َّز َّهُّمْ ق َّق َّذْ ن َّز َّن ِى  وَّصَّلَّ إَّلل َّهُّ مَّن ْ وَّصَّلَّهُّمْ وَّ 
ق َّطَّغَّ إَّلل َّهُّ مَّن ْ ق َّطَّعَّهُّمْ وَّ ن َّصَّزَّ إَّلل َّهُّ مَّن ْ إ َّعَّان َّهُّمْ وَّ ج َّذ َّلَّ إَّلل َّهُّ مَّن ْ ج َّذ َّلَّهُّمْ: إَّلل َّهُّم َّ مَّن ْ كَّان َّ  لَّهُّ مِ ن ْ إ َّن ْن ِي َّان ِك َّ وَّ رُّسُّلِك َّ ي َّق َّل  وَّ 
إ َّهْلُّ ن َّي ْث ٍ ق َّعَّلِى    وَّ ق َّاطِمَّه ُّ وَّ إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ وَّ إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ُّ إ َّهْلُّ ن َّي ْي ِى  وَّ ي َّق َّلِى  ق َّا َّذ ْهِث ْ عَّن ْهُّمُّ إَّلزِّج ْسَّ وَّ  طَّهِّزْهُّمْ ي َّطْهِي زإً
hadiths.en:
Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman narrated from Asim ibn Humayd from Muhammad 
ibn Qays from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}, who said:  
"Indeed, the name of the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) in the scriptures 
of Ibrahim is Al -Mahi (the Eraser), in the Torah of Musa it is Al -Had (the Opponent), in 
the Gospel of Isa it is Ahmad, and in the Qur'an it is Muhammad {saws}." 
It was asked: “What is the meaning of Al-Mahi?"  
Imam {a.s} said: “Al-Mahi is "The one who erases the image of idols, removes the traces 
of false deities, and eliminates every object of worship besides the Most Merciful {SWT}." 
It was asked: “What is the meaning of Al-Had?"  
Imam {a.s} said: “Al-Had is the one who stands in opposition to those who oppose Allah 
{SWT} and His {SWT} religion, whether they are near or far." 
It was asked: “What is the meaning of Ahmad?"  
Imam {a.s} said: “Ahmad means that Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, has praised him 
in the scriptures for the praiseworthy deeds he performed."  
It was asked: “What is the meaning of Muhammad?"  
Imam {a.s} said: “Indeed, Allah {SWT}, His {SWT} angels, all His {SWT} Prophets {a.s}, Messengers 
{a.s}, and all their praise and send blessings upon Him {saws}, and His {saws} name is 
inscribed upon the Throne: 'Muhammad {saws} is the Messenger of Allah {SWT}.'" 
He {saws} used to wear Yemeni caps, white caps, and a wrapped turban with two flaps 
during battles. 
He {saws} had a staff called ‘Anazah, which he would lean on and take out for the two Eid 
sermons. 
He {saws} had a rod called Al-Mamshuq and a tent called Al-Kinn. 
He {saws} had a large wooden bowl called Al-Sa‘ah and a drinking vessel called Al-Riyy. 
He {saws} had two horses: one named Al-Murtajiz and the other named Al-Sakb. 
He {saws} had two mules: one named Al-Duldul and the other named Al-Shahba. 
He {saws} had two camels: one named Al-‘Adhba and the other named Al-Jad‘a. 
He {saws} had two swords: one named Dhu Al-Fiqar and the other named Al-‘Awn. 
He {saws} had two additional swords: one named Al-Mikhdham and the other named Al-
Rasum. 
He {saws} had a donkey named Al-Ya‘fur. 
He {saws} had a turban called Al-Sahab. 
He {saws} had a coat of armor called Dhat Al -Fudul, which had three silver rings - one in 
the front and two in the back. 
He {saws} had a banner called Al-‘Uqab. 
He {saws} had a camel for carrying loads named Al-Dibaj. 
He {saws} had a flag called Al-Ma‘lum. 
He {saws} had a war helmet called Al-As‘ad. 
Upon His {saws}  passing, He {saws} entrusted all of these to Commander of the Faithful 
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}, and placed his ring on Imam Ali’s {a.s} finger. 
Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said that he found a scroll inside 
the hilt of one of the Prophet’s {saws} swords, which contained three statements: 
"Maintain ties with those who cut you off." 
"Speak the truth, even if it is against yourself." 
"Show kindness to those who wrong you." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.177 • Al-Wafi, Vol.3 p.577  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 71, H. 5403

hadiths.chapter: 71. CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS FROM THE TIME OF ADAM
hadiths.ar:
5405  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ِ إِن ْن ِ عَّن  َّاسٍ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ سَّمِعْث ُّ إَّلن  َّن ِى  َّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ي َّق ُّولُّ لِعَّلِى ٍّ عَّلَّي ْهِ  إَّلس َّلاَّم 
وَّصِي ِّى  إ َّوْصَّي ْث ُّ إ ِلَّي ْك َّ ن ِا َّمْزِ رَّن ِّى  وَّ إ َّن ْث َّ ج َّلِي ق َّي ِى  إِسْي َّح ْلَّق ْي ُّك َّ ن ِا َّمْزِ  رَّن ِّى  ي َّا عَّلِى  ُّ إ َّن ْث َّ إَّل َّذ ِى  ي ُّن َّي ِّن ُّ لِأ ُّم َّي ِى  مَّا ي َّح ْي َّلِق ُّون َّ 
ق ِي هِ ن َّعْذِى  وَّ ي َّق ُّومُّ ق ِي هِمْ مَّق َّامِى  ق َّوْلُّك َّ ق َّوْلِى  وَّ إ َّمْزُّك َّ إ َّمْزِى  وَّ طَّاعَّي ُّك َّ طَّاعَّي ِى  وَّ طَّاعَّي ِى  طَّاعَّه ُّ إَّلل َّ هِ وَّ مَّعْصِي َّي ُّك َّ  
مَّعْصِي َّي ِى  وَّ مَّعْصِي َّي ِى  مَّعْصِي َّه ُّ  إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل
hadiths.en:
Al-Mualla ibn Muhammad Al -Basri narrated from Jafar ibn Sulayman from 
Abdullah ibn Al-Hakam from his father from Said ibn Jubayr from Ibn Abbas, who said:  
The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) said: 
"Indeed, Ali {a.s} is my successor and caliph, and his wife (Sayyida) Fatimah {a.s}, my 
daughter, is the leader of the women of the worlds.  
Al-Hasan {a.s} and Al-Hussain {a.s} are the leaders of the youth of Paradise, and they are 
my sons.  
Whoever befriends Them {a.s} has befriended me {saws},  
Whoever opposes Them {a.s} has opposed me {saws}.  
Whoever fights against Them {a.s} has fought against me {saws},  
Whoever distances himself from Them {a.s} has distanced himself from me {saws},  
And whoever honors Them {a.s} has honored me {saws}.  
May Allah {SWT} the Majestic, maintain ties with those who maintain ties with Them {a.s} 
and sever ties with those who sever ties with them {a.s}.  
May Allah {SWT} the Majestic, support those who aid Them {a.s} and abandon those who 
abandon them {a.s}. 
O’ Allah {SWT}! Just as every prophet and messenger had their own household and 
entrusted ones; Ali {a.s}, (Sayyida) Fatimah {a.s}, Al-Hasan {a.s}, and Al-Hussain {a.s} are my 
household and my entrusted ones.  
So remove impurity from Them {a.s} and purify Them {a.s} completely." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.179 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.420 • Al-Amali 
(Lil-Saduq), Vol.1 p.57 • Al-Amali (Lil-Saduq), Vol.1 p.473 • Bisharat Al-Mustafa, Vol.1 p.16 • Al-Wafi, Vol.2 
p.326 • Ithbat Al-Huda, Vol.2 p.38 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.35 p.210 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.37 p.35  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 71, H. 5404

hadiths.chapter: 71. CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS FROM THE TIME OF ADAM
hadiths.ar:
5406   -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ إَّلْكُّوق ِى  ُّ عَّن ْ مُّوسَّى ن ْن ِ عِمْزَّإن َّ إَّلن  َّح َّعِى ِّ عَّن ْ عَّمِّهِ إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ِ   ن ْن ِ ي َّز ِي ذَّ عَّن ِ 
إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ ن ْن ِ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  جَّمْز َّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّن ْ ي َّحْي َّى ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْق َّاسِمِ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّاذِق ِ  ج َّعْق َّزِ ن ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّن ْ ج َّذِّةِ 
عَّلَّي ْهِمُّ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ  إ َّو َّلُّهُّمْ عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  طَّالِث ٍ وَّ 
إ ج ِزُّهُّمُّ إَّلْق َّان ِمُّ ق َّهُّمْ ج ُّلَّق َّا ن ِى  وَّ إ َّوْصِي َّان ِى  وَّ إ َّوْلِي َّان ِى  وَّ جُّح َّح ُّ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّى إ ُّم َّي ِى  ن َّعْذِى  إَّلْمُّق ِز ُّ ن ِهِمْ مُّو ْمِن   وَّ إَّلْمُّن ْكِزُّ لَّهُّ مْ 
كَّاق ِز
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Ibn Abbas that he said:  
I heard the Prophet {saws} say to Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}: 
"O’ Ali {a.s}, you are my successor. I have entrusted to you by the command of my Lord 
{AZJ}, and you are my caliph.  
I have appointed you by the command of my Lord {AZJ}.  
O’ Ali {a.s}, you are the one who will clarify for my nation what they will differ over after 
me, and you will stand in my place among them.  
Your words are my words, your command is my command, obedience to you is 
obedience to me, and obedience to me is obedience to Allah {SWT}.  
Disobedience to you is the disobedience to me, and the disobedience to me is the 
disobedience to Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.179 • Ithbat Al-Huda, Vol.3 p.25  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 71, H. 5405

hadiths.chapter: 71. CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS FROM THE TIME OF ADAM
hadiths.ar:
5407  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ   عِس ْزِي ن َّ إ َّلْف َّ ن َّن ِى ٍّ إ َّن َّا 
سَّي ِّذُّهُّمْ وَّ إ َّق ْص َّلُّهُّمْ وَّ إ َّكْزَّمُّهُّمْ عَّلَّى إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ وَّ لِكُّلِّ ن َّن ِى ٍّ وَّصِ ى    إ َّوْصَّى إ ِلَّي ْهِ ن ِا َّمْزِ إَّلل َّهِ ي َّعَّالَّى ذ ِكْزُّةُّ وَّ إ ِن  َّ وَّصِي ِّى   
عَّلِى  َّ ن ْن َّ إ َّن ِى  طَّالِث ٍ لَّسَّي ِّذُّهُّمْ وَّ إ َّق ْص َّلُّهُّمْ وَّ إ َّكْزَّمُّهُّمْ عَّلَّى إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Abi Abdullah Al-Kufi narrated from Musa ibn Imran Al-Nakhai 
from his uncle Al -Husayn ibn Yazid from Al -Hasan ibn Ali ibn Abi Hamzah from his 
father from Yahya ibn Abi Al-Qasim from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} from 
his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s}, from his grandfather Imam Hussain 
ibn Ali {a.s}, peace be upon them all, who said:  
The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace be upon him and his family) said: 
"The Imams {a.s} after me are twelve.  
The first of them is Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}, and the last of them is the Riser (Al-Qaʾim {a.s}).  
They are my successors, my trustees, my guardians, and the proofs of Allah {SWT} over 
my nation after me.  
Whoever acknowledges them is a believer, and whoever denies them is a disbeliever." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.179 • Uyun Al-Akhbar, Vol.1 p.59 • Kamal Al-Din, Vol.1 
p.259 • Kifayat Al-Athar, Vol.1 p.145 • Kifayat Al-Athar, Vol.1 p.153 • I’lam Al-Wara, Vol.2 p.173 • Jami’ Al-
Akhbar, Vol.1 p.17 • Kashf Al-Ghummah, Vol.2 p.507 • Al-Wafi, Vol.2 p.313 • Ithbat Al-Huda, Vol.2 p.38  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 71, H. 5406

hadiths.chapter: 71. CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS FROM THE TIME OF ADAM
hadiths.ar:
5408   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْح َّارُّوذِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ ج َّان ِزِ ن ْن ِ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ إَّلْأ َّن ْصَّارِى ِّ  
ق َّالَّ : ذَّج َّلْث ُّ عَّلَّى ق َّاطِمَّه َّ عَّلَّي ْهَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ وَّ ن َّي ْن َّ ي َّذَّي ْهَّا لَّوْح  ق ِي هِ إ َّسْمَّاءُّ إَّلْ أ َّوْصِي َّاءِ مِن ْ وُّلْذِهَّا ق َّعَّذَّذْب ُّ إِي ْن َّى ْ عَّس َّزَّ  
إ َّجَّذُّهُّمْ إَّلْق َّان ِمُّ ي َّلاَّي َّه   مِن ْهُّمْ مُّحَّم َّذ  وَّ إ َّرْن َّعَّه   مِن ْهُّمْ عَّلِى    عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ.  
وَّ ق َّذْ إ َّج ْزَّج ْث ُّ إلْأ َّج ْن َّارَّ إلْمُّسْن َّذَّة َّ إلص َّحِي حَّه َّ ق ِى  هَّذ َّإ إلْمَّعْن َّى ق ِى   كِي َّاب ِ كَّمَّالِ إلذِّي ن ِ وَّ ي َّمَّامِ إلن ِّعْمَّه ِ  ق ِى  إ ِي ْن َّاب ِ إلْع َّي ْن َّه ِ وَّ كَّس ْف ِ 
إلْحَّي ْزَّة ِ وَّ لَّمْ إ ُّورِذْ مِن ْهَّا س َّي ْن اً ق ِى  هَّذ َّإ إلْمَّوْص ِغِ لِأ َّن ِّى  وَّص َّعْث ُّ هَّذ َّإ إلْكِي َّاب َّ لِمُّح َّز َّذِ إلْق ِق ْهِ ذُّ ون َّ ع َّي ْزِةِ وَّ إلل َّهُّ إلْمُّوَّق ِّق ُّ لِلص َّوَّإب ِ  وَّ 
إلْمُّعِي ن ُّ عَّلَّى إكْي ِسَّاب ِ إلي  َّوَّإب ِ.
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace be upon him and his family) said: 
"Indeed, Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, has one hundred and twenty-four thousand prophets.  
I am the master, the best, and the most honored of them before Allah {SWT}, the Mighty 
and Majestic.  
Every prophet had a successor whom he appointed by the command of Allah {SWT}, the 
Exalted in His remembrance.  
Verily, my successor, Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}, is the master, the best, and the most honored 
among all of their successors before Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.180 • Qisas Al -Anbiya (Lil-Rawandi), Vol.1 p.372 • Al-
Wafi, Vol.2 p.326 • Ithbat Al-Huda, Vol.3 p.26  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 71, H. 5407
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS FROM THE TIME OF ADAM
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS FROM THE TIME OF ADAM
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧١، ح ٥٤٠٧

hadiths.chapter: 71. CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS FROM THE TIME OF ADAM
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abu Al -Jarud from Abu Jafar Imam 
Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} from Jabir ibn Abdullah Al-Ansari, who said: 
"I entered upon Sayyida Fatimah {s.a}, and before her {s.a} was a tablet in which were the 
names of the successors from her offspring. I counted twelve, and one of them was 
the Riser (Al-Qaʾim {a.s}). Three of them were named Muhammad {a.s}, and four of them 
were named Ali {a.s}." 
[AL SADUQ] 
I have presented the authenticated and well -supported narrations on this subject in 
the book Kamal Al-Din wa Tamam Al -Nimah fi Ithbat Al -Ghaybah wa Kashf Al -Hayrah 
(The Perfection of Religion and the Completion of Blessing in the Proof of Occultation 
and the Unveiling of Perplexity).  
However, I have not included any of them in this place because I have composed this 
book solely for jurisprudence and nothing else. Allah {SWT} is the One who grants 
success in reaching the truth and assists in attaining reward.  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.1 p.532 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.180 • Uyun Al-Akhbar, Vol.1 p.46 • 
Uyun Al-Akhbar, Vol.1 p.47 • Kamal Al -Din, Vol.1 p.269 • Kamal Al -Din, Vol.1 p.311 • Kamal Al -Din, Vol.1 
p.313 • Al-Ghaybah (Lil-Tusi), Vol.1 p.139 • I’lam Al-Wara, Vol.2 p.166 • Jami’ Al-Akhbar, Vol.1 p.17

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 71, H. 5408
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS FROM THE TIME OF ADAM
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS FROM THE TIME OF ADAM
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧١، ح ٥٤٠٨

hadiths.chapter: 72. CHAPTER ON THE FAVOR THAT ALLAH
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Hammad ibn Uthman who said that 
Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said: 
"There is no dying person approaching death except that Allah {SWT} restores to him his 
hearing, sight, and intellect for the purpose of making a will - whether he chooses to 
make the will or not. This is the state referred to as the 'relief of death,' and it is a duty 
upon every Muslim." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.180 • Rawdat Al-Wa’izin, Vol.2 p.482 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, 
Vol.2 p.446 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.100 p.195

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 72, H. 5409
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE FAVOR THAT ALLAH
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE FAVOR THAT ALLAH
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٢، ح ٥٤٠٩

hadiths.chapter: 73. CHAPTER ON ALLAH’S
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Isa ibn Ubayd narrated from Zakariyya Al-Mu’min from Ali ibn 
Abi Nuaym from Abu Hamzah from one of the Imams (peace be upon them), who said: 
"Indeed, Allah {SWT}, the Blessed and Exalted, says:  
‘O’ son of Adam, I have favored you with three things:  
I concealed from others matters about you that, had your family known, they would 
not have buried you.  
I expanded your sustenance, yet when I asked you to lend (in charity), you did not offer 
any good.  
And I granted you a respite at your death in one -third of your wealth, yet you did not 
present any good.’" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.181 • Al-Khisal, Vol.1 p.136 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 
p.175 • Rawdat Al-Wa’izin, Vol.2 p.483 • Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.23 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.2 p.447 • Wasa’il Al-
Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.263 • Kulliyat HADITH Qudsi, Vol.1 p.679 • Kulliyat HADITH Qudsi, Vol.1 p.708 • Bihar Al-Anwar, 
Vol.67 p.19

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 73, H. 5410
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON ALLAH’S
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON ALLAH’S
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٣، ح ٥٤١٠

hadiths.chapter: 74. CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS BEING AN OBLIGATION
hadiths.ar:
5412  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْعَّلاَّءُّ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم ذْ إ َّوْصَّى رَّسُّولُّ 
إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ق َّي َّن ْن َّع ِى  لِلْمُّسْلِمِ إ َّن ْ ي ُّوصِى
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Al -Fudayl narrated from Abu Al -Sabbah Al-Kinani from Abu 
Abdullah Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s}, who said: I asked Imam {a.s} about making a will. 
Imam {a.s} said: "It is a duty upon every Muslim." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.3 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.181 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.2 p.446 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.257 • Ithbat Al-Huda, Vol.1 p.126  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 74, H. 5411
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS BEING AN OBLIGATION
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS BEING AN OBLIGATION
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٤، ح ٥٤١١

hadiths.chapter: 74. CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS BEING AN OBLIGATION
hadiths.en:
Al-Ala’ narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who said that Abu Jafar Imam 
Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said: 
"Making a will is a duty, and the Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace be upon him and his 
family) indeed made a will. Therefore, it is appropriate for a Muslim to make a will."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.3 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.181 • Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.22 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.257 • Ithbat Al-Huda, Vol.1 p.116

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 74, H. 5412

hadiths.chapter: 75. CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS COMPLETING WHAT IS
hadiths.en:
Masadah ibn Sadaqah Al-Rabii narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-
Sadiq {a.s} from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s}, that Imam Ali ibn Abi 
Talib {a.s} said: 
"Making a will completes what is lacking from the payment of zakat."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 4 p.182 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol. 9 p.173 • Tahdhib Al -
Ahkam, Vol.9 p.173 • Rawdat Al-Wa’izin, Vol.2 p.482 • Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.24 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.259 
• Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.259 • Ithbat Al-Huda, Vol.1 p.126 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.100 p.194

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 75, H. 5413
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS COMPLETING WHAT IS
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS COMPLETING WHAT IS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٥، ح ٥٤١٣

hadiths.chapter: 76. CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF ONE WHO MAKES A
hadiths.en:
Al-Sakuni narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Mu hammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} from his 
father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}, that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said: 
"Whoever makes a will without being excessive or causing harm, it is as if he gave it in 
charity during his lifetime." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.62 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.182 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 p.174 
• Al -Wafi, Vol. 24 p.24 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol. 2 p.447 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol. 19 p.264 • Al -Fusul Al -
Muhimmah, Vol.2 p.315 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.100 p.195

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 76, H. 5414
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF ONE WHO MAKES A
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF ONE WHO MAKES A
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٦، ح ٥٤١٤

hadiths.chapter: 77. CHAPTER ON REPORTS REGARDING ONE WHO DOES
hadiths.en:
Abdullah ibn Al -Mughirah narrated from Al -Sakuni from Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s}, who 
said: 
"Whoever does not make a will for his relatives at the time of his death has concluded 
his deeds with disobedience." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.182 • Ithbat Al-Huda, Vol.1 p.126

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 77, H. 5415
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON REPORTS REGARDING ONE WHO DOES
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON REPORTS REGARDING ONE WHO DOES
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٧، ح ٥٤١٥

hadiths.chapter: 78. CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
hadiths.en:
Al-Abbas ibn Amir narrated from Aban from Abu Basir from Abu Abdullah 
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, who said: 
"Whoever does not make a proper will at the time of death, it is a deficiency in his 
dignity and intellect." 
Imam {a.s} also said: "Indeed, the Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace be upon him and his 
family) made a will to Ali {a.s}, and Ali {a.s} made a will to Al -Hasan {a.s}, and Al-Hasan {a.s} 
made a will to Al-Hussain {a.s}, and Al-Hussain {a.s} made a will to Ali ibn Al -Hussain {a.s}, 
and Ali ibn Al-Hussain {a.s} made a will to Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.183 • Al -Wafi, Vol.24 p.26 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.19 
p.265 • Ithbat Al-Huda, Vol.2 p.39

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 78, H. 5416
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٨، ح ٥٤١٦

hadiths.chapter: 79. CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF ONE WHOSE LIFE ENDS
hadiths.en:
Ahmad ibn Al -Nadr Al-Khazzaz narrated from Amr ibn Shimr from Jabir from 
Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s}, who said that the Messenger of Allah 
{SWT} (peace be upon him and his family) said: 
"Whoever's final words are ‘ La ilaha illa Allah {SWT}’ (There is no God but Allah {SWT}) will 
enter Paradise.  
Whoever's life ends while fasting for a day will enter Paradise.  
And whoever's final deed is giving charity sincerely for the sake of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty 
and Majestic, will enter Paradise." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.183 • Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.272 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.19 
p.266 • Al-Fusul Al-Muhimmah, Vol.2 p.315 • Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.14 p.93

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 79, H. 5417
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF ONE WHOSE LIFE ENDS
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF ONE WHOSE LIFE ENDS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٩، ح ٥٤١٧

hadiths.chapter: 80. CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
hadiths.en:
Abdullah ibn Al -Mughirah narrated from Al -Sakuni from Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s}, that 
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"I do not mind whether I cause hardship to my children or I spend that wealth on them." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.183

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 80, H. 5418

hadiths.chapter: 81. CHAPTER ON JUSTICE AND INJUSTICE IN BEQUESTS
hadiths.en:
Harun ibn Muslim narrated from Masadah ibn Ṣadaqah from Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s}, who 
said: 
"Whoever is just in his will is like one who gave it in charity during his lifetime.  
But whoever is unjust in his will shall meet Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, on the 
Day of Judgment while He {SWT} turns away from him." 
[REFERENCES] Qurb Al-Isnad, Vol.1 p.63 • Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.58 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.184 • 
Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.59 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.100 p.197

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 81, H. 5419
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON JUSTICE AND INJUSTICE IN BEQUESTS
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON JUSTICE AND INJUSTICE IN BEQUESTS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨١، ح ٥٤١٩

hadiths.chapter: 82. CHAPTER ON BIAS IN BEQUESTS BEING AMONG THE
hadiths.en:
Harun ibn Muslim narrated from Masadah ibn Ṣadaqah from Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} from his 
forefathers (peace be upon them), who said that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said: 
"Injustice in a will is among the major sins." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.565 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.184 • Ilal Al-
Shara’i’, Vol.2 p.567 • Al-Wafi, Vol.5 p.1056 • Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.60 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.15 p.327 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.268

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 82, H. 5420

reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON BIAS IN BEQUESTS BEING AMONG THE

hadiths.chapter: 83. CHAPTER ON THE RECOMMENDED AMOUNT FOR
hadiths.ar:
5422   -  رَّوَّى جَّم َّاذُّ ن ْن ُّ عِي سَّى عَّن ْ س ُّعَّي ْث ِ ن ْن ِ ي َّعْق ُّوب َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصِي زٍ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إ لس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ 
إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّمُّوب ُّ مَّا لَّهُّ مِن ْ مَّالِهِ ق َّق َّالَّ لَّه
hadiths.en:
Al-Sakuni narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Mu hammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} from his 
father, Imam Mu hammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s}, who said that the Commander of the 
Faithful {a.s} said: 
"Bequeathing one -fifth (khums) is recommended because Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and 
Majestic, has chosen one-fifth for Himself." 
Imam {a.s} also said: "One -fifth is moderation, one -fourth is exertion, and one -third is 
injustice." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.185 • Al -Wafi, Vol.24 p.40 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.19 
p.270  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 83, H. 5421
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE RECOMMENDED AMOUNT FOR
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE RECOMMENDED AMOUNT FOR
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٣، ح ٥٤٢١

hadiths.chapter: 83. CHAPTER ON THE RECOMMENDED AMOUNT FOR
hadiths.ar:
5423  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّاصِمُّ ن ْن ُّ جُّمَّي ْذٍ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ ق َّي ْسٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ كَّان َّ إ َّمِي زُّ إَّلْم و ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ 
إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّق ُّول   إ ُّوصِى َّ ن ِالز ُّن ُّغِ إ َّجَّث  ُّ إ ِلَّى  َّ مِن ْ إ َّن ْ 
إ ُّوصِى َّ ن ِالي  ُّلُّث ِ وَّ مَّن ْ إ َّوْصَّى ن ِالي  ُّلُّث ِ ق َّلَّمْ ي َّي  َّزِك ْ ق َّق َّذْ ن َّالَّغ مْ ي َّي ْزُّك ْ ق َّق َّذْ ن َّلَّغ َّ إَّلْمَّذَّى
hadiths.en:
Hammad ibn ʿIsa narrated from Shuayb ibn Yaʿqub from Abu Baṣir, who said: 
I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a man who dies  - how much of his wealth he can 
bequeath.  
Imam {a.s} said: "One-third of his wealth, and the same applies to a woman as well."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.11 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.185 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 p.191 
• Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 p.119 • Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.38 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.412 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 
p.272  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 83, H. 5422
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE RECOMMENDED AMOUNT FOR
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE RECOMMENDED AMOUNT FOR
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٣، ح ٥٤٢٢
